steps.title: Spot the meaningful contrast
steps.content: The passage establishes that Ibn Rushd was geographically isolated from the Islamic intellectual center. The blank must express a claim about his relationship to other philosophers—specifically, what claim would be meaningful to undermine given this isolation.

steps.title: Match the undermined claim
steps.content:
”Inconsequential to” captures what scholarship undermined: the claim that his works were unimportant to other philosophers. By undermining this claim, it's established that his works actually WERE important despite geographic isolation—a meaningful contrast. The other choices describe different characteristics that don't address intellectual significance or importance.

This matches choice D — D is correct.
